subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Family Communication Plans
description: Learn Family Communication Plans in one minute.

hook.text: After an earthquake, local phone lines jam—but text messages often still get through.

buildup.text: Families separated during a disaster need a pre-agreed way to communicate and reunite.

discovery.text: An out-of-area contact works as a relay—long-distance calls often succeed when local ones fail.

twist.text: Children as young as 4 can memorize a family meeting point and an emergency phone number.

climax.text: Practice the plan twice a year—like a fire drill—so it becomes automatic under stress.

punchline.text: When cell towers fail, your plan becomes your lifeline.

quiz.question: Why does designating an out-of-area contact help during a disaster?
options: Long-distance calls often work when local calls fail
They can call emergency services for you
